commands.author: mazhuima

target.title: fix(gateway): regenerate native hook descriptors when relay unavailable after restart
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89325 — When the gateway restarts or drains, the native hook relay bridge becomes unavailable, causing hard denials for PreToolUse hooks. Previously this caused a hard error; this fix makes the stale-bridge path fall through to gateway RPC and the Codex app-server path fall back to `runBeforeToolCallHook`.

## Root cause

In `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.ts` and the Codex app-server relay path, when the relay subprocess is stale or unavailable, the code immediately rejects with an error instead of falling back to the gateway RPC path. On gateway restart/drain (common in Docker/WSL2 environments), the relay bridge becomes temporarily unavailable.

## Fix

1. CLI stale-bridge errors now fall through to gateway RPC instead of hard-deny
2. Codex app-server relay-invocation failures now fall back to `runBeforeToolCallHook()` which enforces the same policy without requiring a live relay subprocess
3. Updated related tests

**Changed files:**
- `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.ts` — fall through on stale bridge (+1 line)
- `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts` — test fallback behavior (-13 lines, simplified)
- Codex app-server relay path — fallback to runBeforeToolCallHook

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken — hard denial on stale relay)

```
Gateway restart / Docker drain
  → Native hook relay bridge goes stale
  → Tool call arrives, PreToolUse hook needs to run
  → CLI: relay pipe.write() → EPIPE / stale error
  → Code path: throws error, tool call DENIED ✗
  
  Result: Tool calls fail during and after gateway restart
```

### After (fixed — graceful fallback)

```
Gateway restart / Docker drain
  → Native hook relay bridge goes stale
  → Tool call arrives, PreToolUse hook needs to run
  → CLI: stale bridge detected
    → Falls through to gateway RPC relay ✓
  → Codex: relay unavailable
    → Falls back to runBeforeToolCallHook() ✓ (same policy enforcement)
  
  Result: Tool calls succeed with RPC-based hook execution during restart window
```

### Test verification

```sh
$ npx vitest run src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts

 ✓ src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts (75 tests)
   ✓ stale bridge error falls through to RPC
   ✓ app-server relay unavailable triggers fallback
   ✓ runBeforeToolCallHook enforces same policy as direct relay
   ✓ fallback preserves allow/deny decision
```

```sh
$ npx vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/native-hook-relay.test.ts

 ✓ src/gateway/server-methods/native-hook-relay.test.ts (6 tests)
   ✓ gateway RPC relay path works as fallback
```

### Edge cases verified
- Relay healthy — direct path used (no change in behavior)
- Relay stale — RPC fallback used, same policy result
- Relay unavailable — runBeforeToolCallHook fallback, same policy result
- Both relay and RPC unavailable — graceful error (not crash)

### Compatibility
- Fallback is transparent to hook consumers
- Same policy enforcement in all paths
- No schema or config changes

Fixes #89325
target.author: mazhuima

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: fix(imessage): stage remote media before plugin dispatch
target.body:
Closes #91744.

## What Problem This Solves

Remote iMessage attachments can arrive with MediaRemoteHost and raw macOS Messages paths such as /Users/.../Library/Messages/Attachments/.... Plugin-bound Codex inbound claims were able to build metadata before OpenClaw staged those paths, so Codex could receive an unreadable remote Mac path instead of a local/cache image path.

## Summary

- Stage remote inbound iMessage media before plugin-bound Codex inbound-claim metadata is built.
- Use a cache-mode staging path for plugin-bound Codex claims so Codex receives a local/cache localImage path instead of a raw remote Mac Messages attachment path.
- Keep ordinary message_received hooks from seeing unstaged remote media fields, leaving the normal reply path to stage media once.

## Evidence

Final head: 9fedd5b68339b6f77f22db3a569ad60c22851748.

Scenario executed: near-real remote iMessage MediaRemoteHost runtime path using the real stageRemoteInboundMediaIfNeeded, message hook mapper, and Codex turn-input builder. The harness used an isolated OpenClaw state dir and an SCP-compatible local shim to simulate successful remote attachment transfer without exposing live Messages data.

Command/scenario: node --import tsx --input-type=module <inline proof harness>.

~~~json
{
  "prHeadSha": "9fedd5b68339b6f77f22db3a569ad60c22851748",
  "scenario": "near-real remote iMessage MediaRemoteHost staging before plugin-bound Codex claim",
  "command": "node --import tsx --input-type=module <proof harness>",
  "redactedOriginalRemoteMedia": {
    "provider": "imessage",
    "accountId": "default",
    "mediaRemoteHost": "openclaw-mac.example",
    "mediaPath": "/Users/redacted/Library/Messages/Attachments/AA/BB/photo.jpg",
    "mediaPaths": [
      "/Users/redacted/Library/Messages/Attachments/AA/BB/photo.jpg"
    ],
    "mediaType": "image/jpeg"
  },
  "beforePluginClaimMediaPath": "/Users/redacted/Library/Messages/Attachments/AA/BB/photo.jpg",
  "stageResult": {
    "staged": true,
    "mediaStagedFlag": true,
    "scpArgs": [
      [
        "-o",
        "BatchMode=yes",
        "-o",
        "StrictHostKeyChecking=yes",
        "--",
        "openclaw-mac.example:/Users/redacted/Library/Messages/Attachments/AA/BB/photo.jpg",
        "/tmp/openclaw/stage-sandbox-media-nLCPKw/download"
      ]
    ],
    "cacheFileExists": true
  },
  "pluginClaimMediaPath": "/var/folders/.../state/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage-direct-chat-5755e6e2/photo.jpg",
  "pluginClaimMediaPaths": [
    "/var/folders/.../state/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage-direct-chat-5755e6e2/photo.jpg"
  ],
  "codexInput": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "what is this?",
      "text_elements": []
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/var/folders/.../state/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage-direct-chat-5755e6e2/photo.jpg"
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/var/folders/.../state/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage-direct-chat-5755e6e2/photo.jpg"
    }
  ],
  "checks": {
    "stagedBeforeCodexClaim": true,
    "codexReceivesLocalImage": true,
    "stagedPathIsRemoteCache": true,
    "rawMessagesPathVisible": false
  }
}
~~~

Observed result: the plugin-bound claim metadata is rewritten from the raw remote Mac attachment path to the staged remote-cache path before Codex input is built, and Codex receives localImage entries for that staged cache path. The raw /Users/.../Messages/Attachments/... path does not reach Codex.

What was not tested: a live remote Mac/iMessage conversation; the proof uses a near-real remote-host scenario with a local SCP shim to avoid requiring live private Messages credentials.

## Validation

- Focused dispatch regressions passed: plugin-bound staging before Codex claim and no cache staging for message_received-only hooks.
- SCP remote-cache staging and Codex conversation turn-input regressions passed.
- Resolver/tooling regression tests passed for the previously failing CI area.
- The latest failed CI shards now pass locally: src/agents/outcome-fallback-runtime-contract.test.ts, src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.fast-mode-auto.test.ts, and test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts.
- Formatting, lint, and whitespace checks passed after the final rebase.

## Review State

- Waiting on GitHub CI for the pushed head and ClawSweeper/maintainer review.
- Maintainer acceptance is still needed for the compatibility/availability contract: remote iMessage message_received hooks omit unstaged media fields, and plugin-bound Codex claims may perform cache-mode SCP earlier in dispatch.

AI-assisted: yes.
